title.merge-commit: Create merge commit to remote Git repository
title.link-to-existing-repository: Link package to remote Git repository

description.create-new-repository-and-commit: On confirm new remote Git repository will be created and the current package will be linked to it
description.commit: Insert the commit message for Git
description.commit-tag-error: You cannot commit from a package representing a tag. Turn it into a branch first.

merge-commit-message.append: The given message will be appended after the default merge message automatically created by Git

button.advanced-settings: Advanced settings (Keeping the defaults is fine):

checkbox.merge-state-tooltip: If checked then merge state is always created. If unchecked it is created only on conflicts (when we do not match the head of remote branch)

repository-owner-tooltip.name: name
repository-owner-tooltip.can-be: can be either Organization or User (that is it will be equal user's name)

commit.title: Committing
commit.wait-text: ⚠️ Do not modify the committed package during the commit process.

toast.link-success: Successfully updated link to remote Git repository
toast.link-failed: Failed updating link to remote Git repository

error.merge-already-merged: Already up to date - The merge from branch was already merged. We cannot merge again.

publication-branch.title: Publication branch name
publication-branch.tooltip: The publication Git branch, which will contain the generated documentation in the Git repository.

export-format-tooltip.intro: The format in which will be the models exported to Git. The diff editor also shows the models in this format.
export-format-tooltip.json: - JSON is well-tested and the default for the rest of Dataspecer. Therefore, it is recommended.
export-format-tooltip.yaml: - YAML may be slightly more friendly for diff/text viewing experience. However, the implementation is experimental with no guarantee of being bug-free.
export-format-tooltip.note: (From a software perspective we are still working with JSON within Dataspecer, this format only affects the Git export and the diff editor)

export-version-tooltip.old: - The old version maps packages to directories and the models are put as files within the specific package.
export-version-tooltip.new: - The new version is more structured. It still holds true that models are files, which are inside directories that map to packages.
export-version-tooltip.structure: - The structure comes from the fact the models are put to a directory based on a type of the model.
export-version-tooltip.example: - For example, packages are inside "directories" directory, and visual models are in "visual-models" directory.

toast.saved-cache-to-backend: Saved files loaded in cache to backend

button.close-tooltip: Closes the diff editor without saving changes
button.save-all-tooltip: This does save both the changes to files and updates the merge state

tooltip.resolve-conflicts: Resolve all conflicts to enable the button and with that get the option to finalize the merge state. The conflicts are marked as resolved by clicking on checkmarks in the directory diff. This tooltip changes after the button gets enabled.
tooltip.save-and-finalize: First saves all the unsaved changes and then it performs the operation, which triggered the merge state. Can be pull/push/merge.

line.one.part-three: move all changes from left window to right
line.one.part-four: until it has all the changes you want.

left-window.part-four: at the time of creating merge state.

pull.line.two: When finalized the tracked hash in Dataspecer will track the Git one.

line.one.part-three: move all changes from left window to right
line.one.part-four: until it has all the changes you want.

left-window.part-four: at the time of creating merge state.

right-window.part-three: Dataspecer data specification to be pushed.

line.one.part-three: move all changes from left window to right
line.one.part-four: until it has all the changes you want.

right-window.part-two: Merge to - This will be pushed to Git.

merge.line.two: Both left and right contain current contents of data specification stored in Dataspecer.

checkbox.show-stripped-version-tooltip: If checked then strips the datastore of unique fields. If unchecked then it shows the full datastore. Currently only meta files are stripped.

more-detail.title: Diff editor in huge detail:
more-detail.line.one: Note that the short description changes based on the merge state cause
more-detail.line.two: Diff Editor's purpose is to resolve the merge state by performing changes to models and marking conflicts (⚠️) as resolved (✅).
more-detail.line.four: The stripped version of file hides content, which is expected to be changed automatically, such as export time.
more-detail.line.five: The left component of this dialog contains directory diff. The directory diff is visualized with regards to the editable window of the text editor.

color.red.description: node - It is NOT present in the editable window.
color.green.description: node - It is present in the editable window. And not in the other one.
color.blue.description: node - Present in both, but they differ.

more-detail.line.eight: The merge actors are not changed in any way. This means that you have to manually do all the changes if needed.
more-detail.merge-strategy: You can also use merge strategy at the top to do the changes automatically.

line.nine.part-one: adds datastore to the editable window.
line.nine.part-two: removes it. The backend is affected only after clicking on one of the Save buttons.

more-detail.line.ten: The editable window is always on the right.
more-detail.line.eleven: For pull and merge the editable windows are the "merge to" actors.
more-detail.line.twelve: The push is reversed, that is the editable window is the "merge from" actor. This is same as in Git, since the "merge to" is the remote.
more-detail.line.thirteen: Therefore, we have to update the local (the "merge from") to contain the changes from remote and then we can perform the push.
more-detail.line.fourteen: You can move between diffs within the "file" using the buttons at the top or ctrl + 'arrow up', respectively ctrl + 'arrow down'

line-two.part-three: to give Dataspecer Git permissions so you can sync up your Dataspecer package with a Git repository.

description.line-three: Next dialog lets you choose authentication provider.
description.line-four: Hover on the info icon for more info.

buttons.full-public-repo-control: Name + E-mail + Git Push scope (Default sign-in option)
buttons.delete-repo-control: Name + E-mail + Git Push and Delete scope

line-one.part-one: The provided rights, which you are asked further in the sign-in process, are for
line-one.part-two: each repository you have access to

tooltip.line-two: The access token, which comes with the sign-in, should be automatically revoked after you sign out.

short-line-two.part-three: to access all of the important Git features -

short-line-three.part-two: Name + E-mail + Git push + Delete scope
short-line-three.part-three: to get the ability to remove any owned repository

long-line-one.part-seven: to repositories to which you have access.

long-line-two.part-three: rights if you are developer who is debugging Dataspecer's Git integration.

tooltip.long-line-three: It is, of course, accessible to non-developers, but it is discouraged.
tooltip.long-line-five: The access tokens for Git operations are tried out until one succeeds in this order. Your SSH, your PAT, bot SSH, bot PAT
tooltip.long-line-six: where PAT = Personal Access Token, that is the thing you get from the Git provider on sign in.
tooltip.long-subline-one: Fetch issues, pull requests, Git history or similar stuff from the Git provider.
tooltip.long-subline-two: Commit under your name to repositories accessible by the Dataspecer bot.
tooltip.long-subline-three: You can think of a Dataspecer bot as a fallback Git user.
tooltip.long-subline-four: Commit and pull to repositories using your private SSH key, which you can set up once you sign in.

commit-redirect-dialog.title: Are you sure that you want to commit?

suffix.other: opened merge states for current package.
